Charles F. Feeney papers, 1891-2020
Collection Number: 8698

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Charles F. Feeney papers, 1891-2020
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8698
Creator:
Feeney, Chuck, 1931-
Feeney, Chuck, 1931-
Quanitities:
62 cubic feet.
592.1 megabytes.
UQ Honorary Degree films
Digitized Chuck pictures
Content Description

The personal papers of Chuck Feeney.
Collection restricted to the permission of the curator.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Preferred Citation

Charles F. Feeney papers, #8698. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical

Chuck Feeney is a Cornell University alumnus, who graduated from the School of Hotel Administration in 1956 and was a brother of Alpha Sigma Phi and a member of the Sphinx Head Society. Making his fortune in duty free shopping, Feeney began his philanthropic efforts in 1982, with the founding of the Atlantic Philanthropies.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Feeney, Chuck, 1931-
Feeney, Chuck, 1931-
Subjects:
Philanthropists
Charities

Container
Description
Date
Series I. Correspondence
 1959-2011
Processing Information
Titles chiefly aupplied by archivist.
Alphabetical files
 1940-1992
Box 25 Folder 1
80th birthday greetings
 2011-04
Scope and Contents
Packet of faxed and emailed birthday greetings from various individuals including David Kennedy, Frank Mutch, Mike Windsor and others.
Box 25 Folder 2-3
A-C
 1977, 1986-1987, 1989-1998, 2000, 2003-2005, 2007, 2009-2011, 2014-2015
Scope and Contents
Includes correspondence from Michael Bloomberg, Peter Beattie (Premier of Queensland, Australia, 1998-2007) and Bertie Ahern (Taoiseach of Ireland). Letter from Mariko Ando includes photographs. Card from Johanna Boeke contains photographs, including one of Feeney. Letter from Davis Coakley found enclosed a copy of Oscar Wilde: the Importance of Being Irish by Davis Coakley (box 18). Letter from Siobhan Commins found enclosed in a copy of A Secret History of the IRA by Ed Moloney (box 18).
Contents arranged in alphabetical order.

Christmas cards.

Box 25 Folder 11
Cornell University -- various
 1986-1989, 1991-1993, 1996, 2011
Scope and Contents
Folder includes letters from various Cornell offices and organizations. Also present are letters from Duty Free Shoppers Scholarship recipients. Correspondence from President Emeritus Frank H.T. Rhodes filed separately.

Happy birthday from Cornell
DVD-4401

Scope and Contents
Folder includes letters from various Cornell offices and organizations. Also present are letters from Duty Free Shoppers Scholarship recipients. Correspondence from President Emeritus Frank H.T. Rhodes filed separately.

Box 25 Folder 17
Fithian, Peter

Box 25 Folder 18
Fleishman, Joel
 1995, 1997, 2000, 2003
Scope and Contents
Letter from January 11, 2000 was found enclosed a copy of Prime Time: How Baby Boomers Will Revolutionize Retirememnt and Transform America by Marc Freedman (box 35).
Box 25 Folder 19
G-I
 1987-1988, 1990-1993, 1997, 1999, 2001, 2003-2005, 2009, 2011
Scope and Contents
Letter from Sushmita Ghosh and Bill Drayton of Ashoka found enclosed a copy of Leading Social Entrepreneurs Changing the World (box 34). Letters from Dr. David Hill found enclosed a copy of A Humanitarian Submission to Mr. Charles F. Feeney (box 18). Letter from R. Paul Herman found enclosed a copy of How to Change the World: Social Entrepreneurs and the Power of New Ideas by David Bornstein. Folder includes holiday cards from Bill Gates and Michael Higgins, President of Ireland. Letter from Le Ly Hayslip was found enclosed a copy of When Heaven and Earth Changed Places: a Vietnamese Woman's Journey from War to Peace (box 36). Note from Mark Patrick Hederman found enclosed a copy of The Haunted Inkwell (box 36).
Arranged alphabetically by sender's last name.

Bill and Melinda Gates -- letter
 2019-02-12

Harte, Paddy
 1987-1988, 1998, 2010
Scope and Contents
Pamphlet and letter dated 10/12/2010 found enclosed a copy of The Island of Ireland Peace Park and the First World War by Paddy Harte (box 18).

Healy, John
 1989, 2000, 2003
Scope and Contents
Letter dated July 24, 2003 found enclosed a copy of Living, Leading, and the American Dream (box 35).

Helfman, Sarah
 1992-1995, 2000, 2006

Henderson, Gerard

Processing Information

Box 25 Folder 28
J-L
 1981, 1987-1988, 1990-1991, 1996-1998, 2004-2005
Scope and Contents
Note from Gerald Kraak found enclosed in a copy of A Prisoner in the Garden: Opening Nelson Mandela's Prison Archive (box 18). Letter from Jim Kemmy found enclosed a copy of The Limerick Anthology (box 34).
Arranged alphabetically by sender's last name.
Box 25 Folder 29
Karamatsu, Maurice

Box 25 Folder 30-31
Kinney, Desmond

Box 25 Folder 32
Kinney, Desmond
 2005-2006, 2010-2012
Box 25 Folder 33
Kinney, Desmond
 undated, 2000, 2011
Box 25 Folder 34
M-O
 1975, 1987--1993, 1995, 1997-2000, 2003-2005, 2007-2008, 2010-2011, 2014
Scope and Contents
Folder includes pictures of Feeney amd cards from Tom Mitchell. Letter from Betsy McBride found enclosed in a copy of It's a Terrible Day…Thanks Be To God (box 18). Letter from Colin McCrea includes newspaper clipping on Niall O'Dowd's autobiography and was found enclosed in An Irish Voice (box 19). Letter from Paul Murray found enclosed a copy of Agreement! The State, Conflict, and Change in Northern Ireland by Beatrix Campbell (box 18). Letter from Enda O'Coineen found enclosed a copy of the Unsinkable Entrepreneur by Enda O'Coineen (box 18). October 22, 1998 letter from Brendan O'Regan found enclosed in Live Youthfully Now by Russell A. Kemp (box 34).
Arranged alphabetically by sender's last name.

CD-3297

O'Dowd, Niall

Box 25 Folder 38
P-R
 1984, 1988, 1990-1995, 1997-1998, 2001, 2011
Scope and Contents
Includes letter from Cornell President Hunter Rawlings. 1997 letter from T.A. Ryan was found enclosed in copy of 50 Treasures from the Hunt Collection, by Patrick F. Doran (box 18). Letter from Neal Ridge found in enclosed in The Japanese Mind: the Goliath Explained by Robert C. Christopher (box 34).
Box 26 Folder 1
Rhodes, Frank H.T.
 1979, 1988-1993, 1996, 1998, 2003-2004
Box 26 Folder 2
Rolles, Charles
 1970, 1987
Box 26 Folder 3
S-U
 1981, 1986-1988, 1990-1992, 1995-1999, 2003-2004, 2006-2007
Scope and Contents
Card from Johannes Summatavet includes newsclippings, photographs of Feeney in Estonia in 1986. Letters from Ted Thoren include photographs, including ones of Chuck and Helga Feeney. Postcard from Connla (Stokes) found enclosed a copy of Dr. Pollock's Beard: More Recent Stories (box 35). Letter from F.A.O. Schwarz, Jr. (dated April 27, 1999) found enclosed a copy of Wait Till Next Year: a Memoir by Doris Kearns Goodwin (box 35). Letter from Robert H. Schuller found enclosed a copy of Prayer: My Soul's Adventure With God: a Spiritual Autobiography (box 35).
Arranged alphabetically by sender's last name.

Schaefer, Hans
 1997

Letter from Canleth Berry includes photograph of Feeney with Shannon College of Hotel Management students in 1988.

Series II. Business and philanthropy files
Alphabetical files
 1943-2012
Scope and Contents
Alphabetical files on business and philanthropic matters. Included are several files on Cornell University, the Davney Fund, and GAGL (General Atlantic Group Limited).
Feeney kept a number of alphabetical subject files related to his business and philanthropic activities. It is likely that these files were kept at different locations, however there is little evidence to support definitive origins. While Feeney's various alphabetical files remain physically in original order, they have been intellectually integrated in order to better facilitate access by researchers.

Box 13 Folder 18
IRL- Connolly, Frank
 2009-08
Creator: Connolly, Frank
Biographical / Historical
Connolly was an Irish investigative journalist, who at Feeney's behest, produced a plan that resulted in the creation of the Centre for Public Inquiry. He later served as the Head of Communications at the Services Industral Professional and Technical Union in Dublin.

CFF - Ireland visit binders
 1992-1998, 2000, 2002-2003
Scope and Contents
Binders kept by Feeney for Atlantic-related business trips taken to Ireland between 1993 and 2003. Chiefly represented are meeting agendas and background materials with John R. Healy, then the founding managing director of Tara Consultants Ltd (later Atlantic Philanthropies Ireland). Healy served as CEO for the Atlantic Philanthropies from 2001-2007. Also included are meeting materials for the Board of the University of Limerick Foundation, of which Feeney was a member.
Arranged in chronological order.
Processing Information
Unannotated October 2003 Board Book removed. Duplicate of book found in #8540, Box 638, Folder 16; Box 639, Folders 1-2.

Box 29 Folder 21-22
French American Charitable Trust (FACT)
 1991-1996, 2000, 2003
Processing Information
Archivist supplied title.
Biographical / Historical
FACT was established in 1990 by Chuck Feeney and his then wife Danielle. Feeney's daughter Diane was the President of FACT from 1994 until 2013. She, Danielle, and her siblings served on the Board. FACT made its final grant in 2012.

Archivist supplied title.
Biographical / Historical
FACT was established in 1990 by Chuck Feeney and his then wife Danielle. Feeney's daughter Diane was the President of FACT from 1994 until 2013. She, Danielle, and her siblings served on the Board. FACT made its final grant in 2012.

Box 9 Folder 42
IRL - O'Regan, Brendan
 2003
Scope and Contents
O'Regan invented the concept of the airport duty-free shop and established the world's first duty-free shop at Shannon Airport in 1947. He later founded Co-Operation North (now Co-operation Ireland), and the Irish Peace Institute.

Biographical / Historical
Vargo was the president of the US-Ireland Alliance.

Duty Free Shoppers -- general
 1960-2006
Scope and Contents
Files related to Duty Free Shoppers and its subsequent sale to Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy. Of note are Feeney's files on the Wiseman Arbitration.
Files found with no original order. Intellectually arranged in chronological order.
Processing Information
Tax returns removed.

Box 47 Folder 20

 1997-01-10-1997-10-23
The Giving Pledge
 1999-2013
Scope and Contents
Files on the Giving Pledge. Included are materials for gatherings as well as correspondence between Bill Gates, Melinda French Gates, Warren Buffett, and Chuck Feeney.
Feeney arranged these files in roughly alphabetical order.
Biographical / Historical
Founded in 2010 by Warren Buffett, Melinda French Gates, and Bill Gates, the Giving Pledge is a campaign encouraging billionaires to pledge a majority of their wealth towards philanthropy. Feeney officially pledged on February 3, 2011-- almost thirty years after establishing the Atlantic Philanthropies in order to give away his wealth. Other pledgers include Ted Turner, Diane von Furstenberg, and Michael R. Bloomberg.
Box 1 Folder 1
Pledger directory and brochure
 2012-09
Processing Information
Archivist supplied title.
